questions.question_text: A table of values has the column $x=2$, $y=9$. Written as a coordinate, this column is:

options.why: Wrote the pair as $(y,x)$, swapping the two coordinates.
Subtracted the $x$-value from $y$ instead of pairing them.
Replaced the $x$-value with $0$ instead of the given $2$.

questions.question_text: A pattern starts at $5$ when $x=1$ and increases by $2$ each time $x$ increases by $1$. Which table describes it?

options.why: Increased $y$ by $1$ each step instead of the stated $2$.
Used the increase $2$ as the starting value and ignored the start of $5$.
Multiplied by $5$ each step instead of adding $2$.

questions.question_text: Which set of coordinates describes the equation $y=3x+1$?

options.why: Used an increase of $2$, matching only the starting value at $x=0$.
Swapped the coefficient $3$ and the constant $1$ in the equation.
Swapped the $x$- and $y$-value in every ordered pair.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=4,ymin=-1,ymax=7,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4},ytick={0,2,4,6},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.3:3.4,samples=2] {-2*x+6};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,6) (1,4) (2,2) (3,0)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Which table matches the displayed graph?

options.why: Read the line as falling by $1$ each step instead of $2$.
Read the points from right to left, reversing the $y$-values.
Treated the line as rising although the graph falls.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=4,ymin=-1,ymax=10,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4},ytick={0,2,4,6,8,10},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.3:3.4,samples=2] {2*x+3};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,3) (1,5) (2,7) (3,9)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Which equation matches the displayed graph?

options.why: Swapped the increase of $2$ with the starting value $3$.
Read an increase of $1$ although the marked points rise by $2$.
Used the wrong sign for the value where the line crosses the $y$-axis.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=4,ymin=-3,ymax=8,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4},ytick={-2,0,2,4,6,8},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.3:3.6,samples=2] {3*x-2};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,-2) (1,1) (2,4) (3,7)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The equation is $y=3x-2$ and the graph is shown. Which listed coordinate is **not** consistent with this relationship?

options.why: Read one grid line too far from the plotted point at $x=3$.
Miscounted the grid and read the marked point as inconsistent.
Rejected the point on the $y$-axis although the graph passes through it.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=6cm,height=5.4cm,axis lines=middle,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=-1,xmax=6,ymin=-1,ymax=6,xtick={-1,0,1,2,3,4,5,6},ytick={0,1,2,3,4,5,6},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={font=\small,/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={$x$},ylabel={$y$}]\addplot[thick,<->,domain=-0.5:5.5,samples=2] {-x+5};\addplot[only marks,mark=*,mark size=1.8pt] coordinates {(0,5) (1,4) (2,3) (3,2) (4,1)};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A student claims the graph matches $y=-2x+5$ because both pass through $(0,5)$. Which statement is correct?

options.text: They differ: at $x=1$ the graph gives $4$ but $y=-2x+5$ gives $3$.
They match, because they share the point $(0,5)$.
options.why: Assumed one shared point proves two relationships are identical.

options.text: They match, because both are straight lines through $(0,5)$.
options.why: Assumed any two lines meeting at $(0,5)$ must be the same line.

options.text: They differ, because $y=-2x+5$ is not a linear relationship.
options.why: Treated $y=-2x+5$ as non-linear even though it is a straight line.

questions.solution_text:
$x=1:\ -2(1)+5=3$
$3\ne4$
They differ: one shared point at $x=0$ is not enough.
